data.title: CSX/BNSF partnership not a replacement for deal between rails - analyst
data.description:
A partnership between CSX Corp. (NASDAQ:CSX) and Berkshire Hathaways' (NYSE:BRK.B) BNSF isn't a replacement for an eventual combination of the two railroads, according to an Evercore ISI analyst. 

"New service collaborations do not have to be mutually exclusive with an eventual merger,"  Evercore analyst Jonathan Chappell wrote in a note on Friday. "In fact, a successful integration of the new services could end up being a precursor to a deeper end-game collaboration between the two rails. We view the announcement today as confirmation that BNSF and CSX will wait to see how the UNP/NSC regulatory path unfolds from here." 

The announcement comes as investors have been pressuring CSX (NASDAQ:CSX) to do a deal with BNSF so the railroads can effectively compete with a combined Union Pacific (UNP) and Norfolk Southern (NSC). Ancora is pressuring CSX  to explore a merger with either Berkshire Hathaway-owned BNSF or Canadian Pacific Kansas City Southern (CP) and kick-start the process by engaging an investment bank. Failing that, the activist investor is prepared to mount a proxy fight for board seats with the ultimate aim of ousting CEO Joe Hinrichs.

Under the partnership announced on Friday, the two railroad operators will begin a new service between Phoenix, Arizona, and Atlanta Georgia, aiming to convert over-the-road (OTR) freight to rail, as well as a new direct international intermodal service between the Port of New York and New Jersey, and Norfolk, Virginia, and Kansas City.

Shares of CSX fell 3.4% on Friday in the wake of the partnership news, though it appears investors may be overreacting.

data.title: Ob-Gyns break with CDC to back COVID shots during pregnancy
data.description:
The American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists on Friday said it continues to recommend the use of COVID-19 vaccines during pregnancy, becoming the latest physician group to diverge from the U.S. CDC’s guidelines regarding the issue.

Releasing updated considerations for COVID immunizations during obstetric-gynecologic care, the women’s health-focused physician group added that pregnant, soon-to-be pregnant, and lactating women should receive COVID-19 vaccines adjusted for new variants.

Its advisory contrasts with the latest CDC recommendations, which excluded healthy pregnant women and young children from groups eligible to receive COVID shots in May following a brief announcement from Robert F. Kennedy Jr., Health and Human Services Secretary.

"While the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) recently removed its recommendation that pregnant and lactating individuals receive updated COVID-19 vaccines, ACOG's recommendations have not changed," ACOG said.

On Tuesday, the American Academy of Pediatrics announced that all children aged 6–23 months should receive a COVID-19 vaccine, despite CDC recommendations to the contrary.

Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX), Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) and Novavax (NASDAQ:NVAX) manufacture COVID vaccines for the U.S. market.

data.title: Intel in focus as Trump administration may unveil equity stake details soon: report
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) was in focus on Friday as Bloomberg reported the Trump Administration could unveil the details on its equity stake in the beleaguered chipmaker soon.

Details of the announcement could happen as soon as Friday, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the plan.

Intel shares rose nearly 5% in midday trading.

Earlier this month, reports emerged that the U.S. government was discussing taking a 10% stake in the company. Taking a 10% stake in Intel would require about $10.5B and make the U.S. government the largest shareholder. The Administration is considering using funds from the U.S. CHIPS and Science Act to pay for the investment.

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick confirmed this week the Administration was in discussions over using CHIPS funding to invest in Intel. If the U.S. purchased the 10% stake, it would become the company's largest shareholder.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent also said the plan to acquire a stake in Intel to help it develop a functioning foundry domestically is crucial for national security.

The Lip Bu Tan-led Intel recently scored a win when Japan's SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) said it would invest $2B in the company at $23 per share. The company is also reportedly talking to other large investors about taking a stake.

data.title: Cleveland Fed Pres. Hammack: Inflation is too high and trending higher
data.description:
Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack remains firmly focused on combating inflation rather than cutting interest rates, despite market anticipation of a September rate cut following Fed Chair Powell’s recent remarks.

In an interview with CNBC at Jackson Hole, Hammack stressed that inflation remains too high and has been trending upward, creating hardship for lower-income households.

“For me, what the decision really comes down to is that inflation is too high. It’s been trending higher. It puts a real pinch on lower income households,” she said, marking just over one year in her position as Cleveland Fed President. “We’ve been above our target for four years, and we need to get that under control.”

Despite recent downward revisions in employment data, Hammack maintained that the labor market remains healthy with unemployment at 4.2%, which she described as “historically low.”

She indicated that, while she keeps an “open mind” about upcoming policy decisions, her current priority is addressing inflation rather than unemployment, noting that the latter appears to be “around full employment.”

Hammack revealed she believes current monetary policy is only “very modestly restrictive” and estimates that rates are close to the neutral level.

“So, I think we have a very small distance to go to get to neutral,” she said, adding that her estimation of the neutral rate is “one of the higher ones on the committee.”

When asked about calls for Fed Governor Lisa Cook to resign over allegations, Hammack defended her colleague, stating, “I know Lisa Cook to be an outstanding economist and a person of high integrity.”

She also emphasized the critical importance of Fed’s independence in monetary policy decisions to “ensure great outcomes for the American public.”

Hammack concluded by expressing caution about moving too quickly toward accommodative policy, warning that such a shift could “reinvigorate the inflationary pressure.”

More on the Federal Reserve:

  • Powell says shifting risks may warrant rate adjustment
  • The Greenback Is Firm Ahead Of Powell's Jackson Hole Speech
  • Justice Department urges removal of Fed's Cook during probe
  • Atlanta Fed's Bostic continues to view one rate cut this year as appropriate
  • Bessent to start interviewing Fed chair candidates, doesn't back off rate cut argument

data.title: Ross Stores shrugs off tariff pressures, analysts praise value and margin upside
data.description:
Mixed Q2 results and a tariff-clouded outlook weren’t enough to slow down Ross Stores (NASDAQ:ROST), with shares jumping to a three-month high in early Friday trading.

During the company’s earnings call with analysts late Thursday, Ross Stores’ (NASDAQ:ROST) CFO Adam Orvos said that given the uncertainty associated with the macroeconomic environment, the company will maintain a somewhat cautious approach to planning its business for the remainder of the year.

Nevertheless, Wall Street analysts remain bullish on the company’s outlook as Ross’s value proposition will continue to lure in cost-conscious shoppers as competitors struggle to absorb higher costs.

Barclay’s Adrienne Yih: Mitigation efforts, including vendor negotiations, sourcing diversification, and increased penetration of closeout have already helped to offset tariff-related costs. Ross is taking a cautious stance regarding price increases – though management acknowledged some changes had been instituted and that AURs were up “low, low single-digits” mostly from mix not price increases.

Jefferies’ Corey Tarlowe: Ross remains our top pick in off-price given its wide value gap to peers and the margin opportunity that lies ahead. We view ROST as well-positioned to outperform as comparable sales should accelerate. We see EBIT expansion over the next 3 years and value upside ahead.

Wells Fargo’s Ike Boruchow: Our Overweight rating on ROST reflects its more defensive nature, downside support on valuation, and levers in place to sustain/beat current street forecasts. While ROST is clearly dealing with more volatility than is typical for their model, at present, valuation is near-trough levels and we expect a much better relative performance in a slower macro tape.

Citi Research’s Paul Lejuez: This quarter again shows the off-price model and ROST are well-positioned in the current environment.

data.title: ATCO wins regulatory approval for Yellowhead pipeline project
data.description:
Canadian energy infrastructure developer ATCO (OTCPK:ACLLF) said Friday it received approval from the Alberta Utilities Commission on its needs assessment application for the Yellowhead pipeline project.

Yellowhead is expected to expand the capacity of Alberta's natural gas transmission system, spanning 230 km from the Peers area to Fort Saskatchewan to deliver more than 1.1B cf/day of natural gas, underpinning the province's economic and population growth.

The need assessment application is the first of two key regulatory filings that require approval from the AUC to advance the project; ATCO (OTCPK:ACLLF) said it plans to file a separate application later this year to seek AUC approval for construction of the infrastructure, which is expected to begin in 2026.

data.title: Cybersecurity company Netskope files for IPO
data.description:
• Despite an industry that is increasingly being dominated by CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD), Palo Alto Networks (PANW) and other big players, cybersecurity company Netskope filed to go public on Friday.
  • Set to trade on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "NTSK," said it generated $707M in annual recurring revenue as of July 31, up 33% year-over-year. Revenue increased 31% year-over-year to $328M for the six months ended July 31, 2025, the company added.
  • It had a net retention rate of 118% and more than 85% of its annual recurring revenue is coming from customers who spent $100,000 or more, the company said in its S-1 filing. Its cash flow in the first-half of fiscal 2026 was $9M and it generated a net loss of $170M during the same time period.
  • Leading the offering will be Morgan Stanley and J.P. Morgan. Other banks such as BMO Capital Markets, TD Cowen, Citizens Capital Markets, Mizuho, Wells Fargo Securities, Deutsche Bank Securities, Oppenheimer, BTIG, KeyBanc Capital Markets, Piper Sandler, William Blair, Santander and Credit Agricole CIB are also participating.

data.title: Option Care Health rises after insider stock purchase
data.description:
• Option Care Health (NASDAQ:OPCH) shares rose after Directors of the company bought about $1.6M worth of shares.
  • David Golding, Timothy Sullivan, Harry Kraemer and Carter Pate bought a total of 57,750 shares of the company in between the price range of $21.15 per share and $27.62 per share.
  • The stock is up 5% today.

data.title: Ventas said to acquire senior housing portfolio on Long Island for $600M
data.description:
Ventas (NYSE:VTR) is acquiring a portfolio of six Long Island-based senior communities from Harrison Street Asset Management and B2K Development for over $600M, according to a media report published Friday.

“We continue to capitalize on the compelling secular megatrend of a large and growing aging population with this attractive acquisition and new relationship with a well-regarded senior housing operator,” Ventas Chief Investment Officer Justin Hutchens told Bloomberg in an emailed statement.

At the end of June, Ventas' (NYSE:VTR) senior housing portfolio encompassed more than 850 facilities. 

B2K will remain responsible for overseeing the six properties, with the potential to collect performance-based fees, the article said.

VTR shares slipped 0.5% in midday trading.

Ventas (VTR) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment on the timing of the deal.

data.title: European indexes uptick cautiously as markets digest EU-U.S. trade deal
data.description:
Update: Update: The post reflects European markets closing indices.

London (UKX) +0.13% to 9,321 following a record close above 9,300 the previous day.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.29% to 24,363. Germany's GDP expands 0.2% Y/Y in Q2

France (CAC:IND) +0.40% to 7,970. 

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) turned 0.47% higher to 561.69 on Friday, as investors turned cautious ahead of Fed Chair Jerome Powell’s Jackson Hole speech and weighed the impact of the EU’s new trade deal with the U.S. EU pledged $750B in U.S. energy spending and at least $600B in investment, securing a tariff cap of 15% instead of the threatened 30%. Pharmaceuticals will face 15% levy.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 6 basis points at 4.26%.

Germany's 10-year yield was unchanged at 2.72%.

UK's 10-year yield was unchanged at 4.69%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

Seeking Alpha Editor Khushi Singh contributed to this article.
